data.title: Bitcoin's On-Chain Losses Suggest Reduced Selling Pressure
data.description: According to PANews, cryptocurrency analyst Ali Martinez revealed on the X platform that Bitcoin's on-chain traders are experiencing an average loss rate of 13.86%. Historically, this level indicates that selling pressure may be diminishing.

data.title: Polygon Price To Drop To $0.04 Amid Bearish Market Outlook?
data.description:
Polygon price has recently given indications that it may just be entering a bear market. The price has failed to hold several crucial support levels support and this has led some analysts to expect the cryptocurrency to drop to $0.04. Polygon Price Breaks Below $0
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/03/22/polygon-price-to-drop-to-0-04-amid-bearish-market-outlook/

data.title: Nick Tomaino: Ethereum has become the dominant ecosystem for tokenizing real-world assets
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Nick Tomaino, founder of 1confirmation, said in an article that the tokenization of real assets (RWA) is accelerating, covering multiple fields such as government bonds, stocks, bonds, private credit, commodities, real estate, etc. Among them, Ethereum has become the dominant ecosystem for the tokenization of real assets.

data.title: Ethereum Leads in Real-World Asset Tokenization
data.description: According to Odaily, Nick Tomaino, founder of 1confirmation, has stated that the tokenization of real-world assets (RWA) is rapidly advancing. This trend encompasses various sectors, including government bonds, stocks, bonds, private credit, commodities, and real estate. Ethereum has emerged as the leading ecosystem for the tokenization of these real-world assets.

data.title: This Bitcoin Correction Is Different From March 2024—Here’s Why
data.description:
An analyst has explained how this Bitcoin correction phase may be different from the 2024 one, based on the data of this on-chain metric. Stablecoin Supply Is Displaying A Different Pattern In This Bitcoin Downturn In a CryptoQuant Quicktake post, an analyst has shared about how the latest trend in the stablecoin circulating supply has been looking. “Stablecoins” are cryptocurrencies that track the price of a fiat currency, with USD being by far the most popular choice. These tokens run on multiple networks, but in the context of the current topic, only the Ethereum-based ones are of interest
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoin-correction-different-march-2024-why/

data.title: Movement Network announces postponement of MoveDrop to optimize ecosystem integration and security audit
data.description:
According to the official Discord of Movement Network, MoveDrop will be postponed to ensure the full integration of Cornucopia, Parthenon v2 and MoveDrop and the completion of security audits.
The team said that the delay of MoveDrop is to ensure the stability and security of the ecosystem. The current focus includes Cornucopia, which will provide more than $250 million in liquidity to drive the growth of the ecosystem; Parthenon v2, whose community reward system will usher in a major upgrade to optimize the user experience; and MoveDrop, which will further optimize the reward distribution method to enhance the network effect.
Currently, the Movement Foundation is completing the mainnet bridge of Cornucopia funds, and the DApp and NFT teams are waiting for the release of liquidity to launch the project. At the same time, the major upgrade of Parthenon v2 still needs further integration.

data.title: Ethereum Spot ETFs Experience Significant Outflows
data.description: According to Odaily, data from SoSoValue indicates that Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $18.63 million on March 21, Eastern Time. The largest single-day net outflow was recorded by BlackRock's ETF ETHA, amounting to $11.93 million, with its historical total net inflow reaching $4.073 billion. Grayscale's ETF ETH followed with a net outflow of $6.692 million, and its historical total net inflow stands at $577 million. As of the latest report, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $6.769 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio of 2.84% compared to Ethereum's total market value. The cumulative historical net inflow has reached $2.417 billion.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETF had a total net inflow of 83.0919 million US dollars yesterday, continuing its net inflow for 6 consecutive days
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (March 21, Eastern Time), the total net inflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was 83.0919 million US dollars.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net inflow in a single day yesterday was BlackRock Bitcoin ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of 105 million US dollars in a single day. Currently, IBIT's total net inflow has reached 39.774 billion US dollars.
The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest net outflow in a single day yesterday was Grayscale Bitcoin ETF GBTC, with a net outflow of 21.8972 million US dollars in a single day. Currently, GBTC's total net outflow has reached 22.526 billion US dollars.

As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was 94.351 billion US dollars, the ETF net asset ratio (market value to the total market value of Bitcoin) reached 5.65%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached 36.048 billion US dollars.
